programmes.show_description: A young model is lured to a secluded prison where a sadistic matron punishes women for perceived moral failings. Contains nudity and distressing scenes.
Scientists investigate a remote island plagued by mutations caused by toxic waste, uncovering a disturbing government cover-up.
A history of London's Session Musicians, sharing their memories and experiences from the 50s and 60s onwards across the musical spectrum, from rock and pop to reggae and classical.

11/35. Miranda and Andrew's garden in Ledbury is a mass of concrete, devoid of plants. They would love an Italian courtyard, reminiscent of their 25th wedding anniversary in Tuscany
3/3. Alan is in Chester to meet retirees Richard and Ann, who have been married for 34 years.
8/8. A piece of Ayrton Senna's race car and a poster from a Sex Pistols tour that never happened are some of the rarities up for grabs this time. Last in series.
1/15. In the Lake District, Rick shares mutton hot pot with sheep farmer and author James Rebanks. He meets an intrepid shrimp fisherman and cooks his mum's shepherd's pie.
1/15. Former footballer Les Ferdinand is coerced into the North Sea in a wetsuit as he joins Robson Green on a short break in Northumberland packed with memories and laughter.
Tonia is on her ancestral homeland of Cyprus. There she visits a goat farm to help milk some lively goats and to see how traditional halloumi cheese is made.(S1/E1)
Relive the glory days of good clean Hi-De-Hi style fun with this oral history of Britain's holiday camps. Hello Campers!
3/8. Seasoned floating homeowners Pat and Sue plan to spend their retirement on a state-of-the-art Dutch barge. But will their expensive tastes sink the project?
3/3. Alan is in Chester to meet retirees Richard and Ann, who have been married for 34 years.
1/5, The estate gets ready for Easter, as castle manager John organises an egg hunt and head of visitor reception Paul prepares the vintage Rolls-Royce. Will the first event of the season go to plan?

programmes.show_name: Can You Keep It Up For A Week?
programmes.show_description: Comedy. Annette will marry Gil, her hapless boyfriend, on condition he keeps his new job for a week without getting up to mischief with other women. Contains sexual scenes.
A young orphan girl Justine falls into a world of cruel depravity while her sister Juliette thrives in vice. Adaptation of de Sade's novel. Contains distressing scenes and nudity.

1/3. Alan Titchmarsh and the team help a 15-year-old boy confined to his wheelchair who has become increasingly confined to his house and cannot access the garden.
2/15. In Argyll, Rick meets a chef who cooks local and foraged ingredients. He catches langoustine with a teacher-turned-fisherwoman and makes tattie scones.
2/15. Robson and road trip buddy, actor Mark Benton, go on a humour-filled quest to find ways to relax while exploring the North Yorkshire coast.
Tonia travels to Mykonos, one of the chicest resorts in Greece. There she cooks red mullet parcels, and baklava - a traditional sweet Greek dessert made with nuts and honey. (S1/E2)
Richard returns to Scotland to embark on tour of the Highlands and Islands. The journey offers him the opportunity to discover more about his homeland and reflect on his life.
4/8. In Surrey, Linda and Mark need an upgrade on their cramped floating home and Linda's doing it by herself. Is passion enough to get the job done?

2/35. Charlie and Lee are in Cwmbran to create a relaxed, free-flowing garden to celebrate Jane's all-clear from cancer.
Comedy. A private eye's assistant tries to prove himself whilst the boss is away - solving a case involving blackmail, a spooky house, a mobster and a singer. Contains sex scenes.
